svg.title: ML Canvas — AttnRes
svg.desc: Machine Learning Canvas for the Attention Residuals project, showing value proposition, data sources, learning problem, predictions, features, decisions, evaluation, building, and monitoring blocks.

g.onclick: sendPrompt('Tell me more about the AttnRes value proposition')
g.text: 20% compute cost reduction · same quality at 0.8× training budget · drop-in residual replacement · <2% inference overhead
Closes the PreNorm dilution gap: bounded hidden-state magnitudes at any depth · selective, learned depth aggregation

g.onclick: sendPrompt('What data sources does AttnRes use?')
sendPrompt('What is the core learning problem AttnRes solves?')
g.text: Input: image patch sequence (B, T, d)

g.onclick: sendPrompt('What features does AttnRes use as model inputs?')
sendPrompt('What decisions are triggered by AttnRes predictions?')
sendPrompt('How is the AttnRes model built and trained?')
sendPrompt('How is AttnRes evaluated offline and online?')
g.text: baseline vs AttnRes vs Full AttnRes

g.onclick: sendPrompt('How is the AttnRes model monitored in production?')
sendPrompt('Where does ML add the most value in AttnRes?')
g.text: Adding ML — where the model matters
The α_{i→l} attention weights are learned end-to-end: no hand-crafted residual schedule, no fixed gate — the network discovers which depth levels
to trust for each token and position. This replaces a human design decision (fixed unit weights) with a data-driven one (softmax over depth).

g.onclick: sendPrompt('What is the business impact of AttnRes?')
g.text: Match baseline quality at 0.8× compute · <2% inference overhead · all benchmarks improve · fully reproducible experiment pipeline
